mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed cases, or interaction term coefficient turns negative in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production reflect weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not precede negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, with directional accuracy falling below 45% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or inflation surprises show zero predictive power for returns.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue baselines, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% while personal consumption expenditures stagnate signals a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-consumption divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with directional accuracy dropping below 40% during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in industrial production above its recent trend by more than 0.5% indicates supply-side momentum that triggers algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production breakouts do not generate positive 5-day returns in recovery regimes, with win rate falling below 45% in out-of-sample tests.
